title: Voter Import Tutorial
subtitle: Learn how to bulk-register organisation members as voters for an election.

section_overview.heading: Overview
section_overview.body: The voter import feature lets election administrators register multiple organisation members as voters in one step by uploading a CSV file. Only members who already exist as active formal members of the organisation can be registered.

section_requirements.heading: Who Can Be Imported as a Voter?
section_requirements.intro: Before importing, the person must fulfil all three conditions:
conditions: They must have a user account on the platform (a registered email address).
They must be a formal member of this organisation — present in the Members list.
Their membership status must be active (not expired, suspended, or ended).
section_requirements.warning: Emails that do not meet all three conditions will be automatically skipped during import. No error will stop the import — valid rows are always processed.

section_file_format.heading: CSV File Format
section_file_format.intro: The import file must be a CSV with semicolon (;) as the delimiter.
rules: The first row must be a header row containing the column name: email
Each subsequent row must contain exactly one email address.
The file must be saved in UTF-8 encoding.
section_file_format.download_hint: Use the Download Template button on the import page to get a ready-to-use CSV file.

steps.title: Step 1 — Prepare your CSV file
steps.body: Make sure all email addresses in your file belong to active formal members of this organisation. Download the template and fill it in.

steps.title: Step 2 — Upload the file
steps.body: On the import page, click "Select File" or drag and drop your CSV file into the upload area.

steps.title: Step 3 — Review the preview
steps.body: The system will validate each row and show a preview table. Valid rows show ✅, invalid rows show ❌ with the reason. Invalid rows will be skipped — they do not block the import.

steps.title: Step 4 — Confirm and register
steps.body: Click the "Register voters" button. The system will add all valid rows as voters for this election. You will see a summary of how many were registered, how many were already existing voters, and how many were skipped.

items.question: What happens if an email is already registered as a voter?
items.answer: It is counted as 'already existing' and not duplicated. The import is safe to run multiple times.

items.question: What happens if a person is a platform user but not a member of this organisation?
items.answer: They will be skipped. Only active formal members with full voting rights can be registered as voters.

items.question: Can I import voters from outside the organisation?
items.answer: No. The import is intentionally restricted to existing active members to protect election integrity.

items.question: The import says 0 registered — what went wrong?
items.answer: The most likely reasons are: the emails do not belong to active members of this organisation, or the CSV column header is missing or misspelled. Download the template and verify your data.

items.question: Can I use a comma-separated CSV instead of semicolon?
items.answer: The system auto-detects the delimiter (comma or semicolon), so both formats are accepted.

seo.title: Voter Import Tutorial | Public Digit
seo.description: Learn how to import voters for an election on Public Digit. Step-by-step guide covering file format, membership requirements, and the import process.
seo.keywords: voter import, election voters, CSV import, bulk import, member voters
